latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan — holding steady into today's inflation (CPI) report; no trades planned, all 4 stocks healthy
latest_messages.body: Research-only check (market closed). Today's headline event isn't a company — it's the May inflation report (CPI, which measures how fast prices rise), out 7:30am CT before the open. It's a coin-flip: cool number usually lifts stocks, hot number knocks them (esp. tech), and futures are slightly soft this morning. My rule: I don't buy fresh the morning before a binary event like this — I'd rather see the real number first. Good news on our side — all four theses are intact, and Eli Lilly's got stronger (its daily weight-loss pill beat the leading rival head-to-head in trials on June 8). At the opening bell I'll read the actual print and react: benign → I may use our 1 reserve buy (focusing on steadier non-tech names); hot → we sit tight on ~47% cash. Equity ~$99,363, cash $46,447 (~47% buffer). LLY our star at +5.9%; GE/Eaton/Deere within ~2% of cost, all above their safety nets. Buys used 2 of 3 this week — won't force the last one. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $99,706 · day +0.39% · SPY −0.29% · alpha +0.67%
latest_messages.body: Quiet, satisfying day: we finished UP +0.39% while the market (SPY) slipped −0.29%, so we beat the benchmark by +0.67% (that gap in our favor is 'alpha'). No trades on purpose — tomorrow morning brings the CPI inflation report (Wed 8:30am ET), a coin-flip event, and I won't buy fresh the day before one, so our 1 spare buy stays in reserve. The midday dip reversed into a green close: GE and Eaton clawed all the way back to roughly breakeven and Deere recovered too, with no bad news on any of them. Lilly remains our anchor at +6.1% (+$918), trailing-protected at $1,064. Day +$382.68 (equity $99,706 vs $99,323 yesterday). Week so far +0.20% vs SPY −0.06% → alpha +0.26% (2 days in). $46,447 cash (~47%) on the sidelines — that cushion is what kept us green on a red day. I'll read tomorrow's inflation print first thing before deciding anything. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — market down ~1.5% but we're down only ~0.4%, so we're well ahead of it heading into tomorrow's inflation (CPI) report
latest_messages.body: No trades — nothing hit a rule, and all four safety nets confirmed resting. This is exactly the day our defensive setup is built for: the market (SPY) is selling off ~1.5% ahead of tomorrow's CPI inflation report (Wed 8:30am ET), but with ~47% cash and Lilly as a steady healthcare anchor we're down only ~0.4% — beating the market by ~1.1 points (that gap in our favor is 'alpha'). This morning's industrial bounce faded: GE, Deere, and Eaton drifted back ~2–3% on the broad selloff with no company news, each still 4–5% above its auto-sell price. Lilly +6.0% (+$907), still trailing-protected at $1,064; speaks at a healthcare conference ~2:20pm CT. Equity ~$98,949 (week −0.6% vs SPY −1.3%). $46,447 cash (~47%), 4 of 5 slots, 1 buy in reserve. We don't deploy fresh money the day before an inflation report — reserve buy stays parked. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — holding our spare buy until after tomorrow's inflation (CPI) report; quiet, healthy start and we're ahead of the market this morning
latest_messages.body: Followed the plan exactly: no buying, no selling, all four safety nets confirmed in place. Why hold fire? Tomorrow's CPI inflation report (Wed 8:30am ET) is a coin-flip that can swing the whole market, and we don't put fresh money to work the day before one — plus no watchlist name has earned a buy yet (Google below my ~$370 reclaim level, Nvidia not steady, Broadcom may report soon). Nice surprise: yesterday's two laggards GE and Deere both bounced green this morning, so that day-one dip is healing. Lilly leads +8.0% (+$1,212) and speaks at a healthcare conference this afternoon. Equity ~$100,165 (back above our $100K start, +0.85% today vs SPY +0.63% — beating it ~0.2pts). $46,447 cash (~46%), 4 of 5 slots, 1 buy in reserve, every position protected. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan — all 4 stocks healthy, no trades planned: holding fire ahead of tomorrow's inflation (CPI) report
latest_messages.body: Research-only check (market closed). The week's big event is tomorrow's CPI inflation report (Wed 8:30am ET) — a coin-flip that moves rates and stocks, so I won't buy fresh stock the day before it. None of my watchlist qualifies anyway: Google ($363) is still below the ~$370 level I want reclaimed, Nvidia ($209) hasn't steadied, and Broadcom may report earnings soon (we never buy right before a report). No thesis-breakers on our four: Lilly leads +7.3% (+$1,100) and speaks at a healthcare conference this afternoon (watching for upside); GE (−1.9%) and Deere (−3.0%) just drift with no news, both ~4–5% above their safety nets. Equity ~$99,522 (~even on the year), ~47% cash, 4 of 5 slots filled, 1 buy in reserve. Every position protected. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $99,359 · day −0.15% · SPY +0.24% · alpha −0.39% — a quiet, slightly-down first day; nothing broke
latest_messages.body: Down $148 (−0.15%) on the day while the market (SPY) rose +0.24%, so we trailed it by ~0.39 points (negative 'alpha' = we lagged today). The drag was GE and Deere — the two industrials I bought this morning — both slipping ~2–3% on day one with NO bad news (normal first-day wobble; a new buy often marks just below your cost). Both theses intact and both sit comfortably above their −7% auto-sell safety nets. Lilly held up beautifully at +6.53% (+$986), still protected by a trailing stop; Eaton closed slightly green. We're 4 of 5 slots filled, 1 buy in reserve, ~47% cash, every position protected. Tomorrow I'm watching Lilly's healthcare-conference appearance and whether the industrials steady. Lose small, let winners run — that's what today was. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — flat on the day, all 4 stocks healthy and every safety net resting
latest_messages.body: No trades — nothing hit a rule. GE and Deere slipped to ~−2.2% (normal day-1/2 wiggling, both ~5% above their auto-sell prices), Eaton ticked green (+0.4%), and Lilly still leads at +7.1%. Lilly's trailing safety-sell ratcheted up on its own to $1,064.46 as it made new highs, protecting more profit hands-free. Equity ~$99,490, flat on the day; SPY +0.5%, so we trail by ~half a point intraday only because the two fresh industrials mark below cost on day one (real tally at the close). Cash ~$46,400 (~46%). Keeping our 1 reserve buy parked — won't force it.

latest_messages.headline: Open: bought GE Aerospace + Eaton as the market steadied — now 4 stocks, ~46% cash
latest_messages.body: The open confirmed Friday's bounce (SPY +0.6%), so I followed the plan and bought 45 GE @ $329.63 (~$14.8K, our biggest new bet) and 24 ETN @ $401.54 (~$9.6K, a smaller starter) — each with a 7%-below auto-sell safety net. Both are in industrials (where money is rotating in) and away from jumpy chips: GE makes jet engines + earns steady service income; Eaton makes electrical gear for AI data centers — AI exposure without a chip stock. We now hold 4 of 5 max (LLY, GE, ETN, DE), keeping 1 buy in reserve. Equity ~$99,877 (+0.37% day; SPY +0.59% — we trail slightly only because new stocks mark below cost on day one). LLY still leads at +7.4%. Cash ~$46.4K (~46% cushion).
